source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Carl writes down a five-digit number.
He then places a shape on each of the five digits (see picture).
He places different shapes on different digits.
He places the same shape on the same digits.
Which number did Carl hide?
<image1>

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a different image reference ('images/146.jpg') than the top-level image_paths entry.

multi_step: question asks to recover a hidden five-digit number rather than read off a visible label
question_text provides at least two linked constraints: different shapes mark different digits, and the same shape marks the same digit
solving likely requires combining visual shape placements with digit-equality constraints across multiple positions
choices is empty, so the task is not reduced to simple answer selection
verifiability: the stated shape-digit consistency rules give a structured basis for checking whether a proposed number matches the visual pattern

risk_flags: High image dependence with no image content transcribed into text.
metadata.image_paths contains a different image reference ('images/146.jpg') than the top-level image_paths entry.

decision_reasons: Question, image asset, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantics are still recoverable.
The sample is highly image-dependent, and the image content is not transcribed into text, which makes downstream use more fragile.
There is an image-reference inconsistency between metadata.image_paths and the top-level image entry, so the asset linkage should be verified before higher-priority inclusion.
